characters.name: Alonzo Solace
characters.description: Alonzo SolacePortrayed byAntonio Sabàto, Jr.FirstappearanceFirst ContactThe pilot for the EdenAdvance Ship.Alonzois a cocky, good-looking, arrogant womanizer. He considered the best pilot around. Because he has been on so many cold-sleep runs, he is 109 years old, but looks like he's in his early twenties. Also due to spending time in cold-sleep, he doesn't dream. In the crash, he breaks his leg while shielding Julia from the lifepod's impact. He spends much of the early part of the trip doped up on painkillers. In his sleep, he is contacted on the Dream Plane by the Terrians, who use him as their spokesman. During the course of the season, he becomes less arrogant and more in tune with his spiritual nature. He also forms a deep commitment to Julia - much more so than he would have on the Stations. Of all the characters, Alonzo is the most changed by his time onG88-9.

characters.name: Baines
characters.description: BainesPortrayed byRockmond DunbarBaineswas a member of the bridge crew on theAdvance Ship. He's a background character with few lines, but since the cast is limited to the 2 or 3 dozen survivors of the crash he appears in most episodes of the series for continuity. He knows how to handle a rifle so is often on guard duty, he can usually be seen during standoffs withGrendlersandTerrians.

characters.name: Bess Martin
characters.description: Bess MartinRelativesMorgan Martin(Husband)Unnamed FatherPortrayed byRebecca GayheartFirstappearanceFirst ContactThe Government Liaison,Morgan Martin, and his Earth-raised wife, 'Bess, are the only married couple on G889. They were married approximately two years when they left the Stations. While total opposites, Morgan and Bess are deeply committed to each other. Unnamed Father Bess, the daughter of an Earth miner, was uncomfortable with life on the Stations.

characters.name: Commander Broderick O´Neill
characters.description: Commander Broderick O´NeillPortrayed byRichard BradfordFirstappearanceFirst ContactBroderick O'Neill is a retired military man who is technically in charge of Eden Project. He is Devon's right-hand man. He looks upon G889 as a great adventure, and the planet is his to conquer. O'Neill is "killed" when he shoots at aKoba, and the Koba shoots its claw at him. He was buried, then dug up byGrendlerswho were subservient toGaal. When the colonists discovered that Koba stings merely render a victim comatose, they returned to his gravesite. Later, O'Neill is investigating Gaal's lair when Gaal sends several Grendlers to kill him (for real this time).

characters.name: Devon Adair
characters.description: Devon AdairRelativesUlysses Adair(son)Portrayed byDebrah FarentinoFirstappearanceFirst ContactDevon Adair is the leader of the Eden Expedition. Her son Ulysses Adair is afflicted with the Syndrome. As a leader, she attempts to balance navigating the colonists' obstacles while confronting the possibility that her son may not survive his sickness. Devon inherited her wealth from her father's work on the space stations. Her mother died when she was 18. Later in her life on the stations, she gave birth to Uly, whose sickness drove her to found a place where children with such condition could thrive. After the crash, Devon continued her leadership role and encouraged the group to travel to New Pacifica, their original destination.

characters.name: Gaal
characters.description: GaalRelativesUnknownPortrayed byTim CurryFirstappearanceFirst Contact at the End of Part 2Gaalwas a penal colonist who claimed to have killed at least 20 People on thestations. He was sent toG88-9as part of theE-2program. There, he claimed to have killed at least 6 other penal colonists. Commander Broderick O Neill was killed by hisGrendlerswhile investigating him. It is later revealed that he has taken control of terrians through shock collars. True Danziger, who has believed in him up until this point, realizes his true nature when she sees this. True steals the necklace Gaal uses to protect himself from the terrians and leaves him to his fate.

characters.name: John Danziger
characters.description: John DanzigerRelativesTrue Danziger(daughter)Portrayed byClancy BrownFirstappearanceFirst ContactHead of the Operations crew for EA,John Danzigerwas not supposed to land on the planet. He is a solid, blue-collar man who is a gifted mechanic. He lived in the Quadrant, which was presumably the working-class ghetto of the Stations. He was also working to pay off the debt of his grandparents' passage up to the Stations from Earth that he'd inherited from his parents. As a result, he has very little respect for authority, especially that of his "betters" on the Stations. He knows that his skills are needed if the group is to make it toNew Pacifica, and doesn't let you forget it.

characters.name: Julia Heller
characters.description: Julia HellerRelativesCouncilwoman HellerPortrayed byJessica SteenFirstappearanceFirst ContactJuliais the only doctor on board theAdvance Ship, and the youngest member of the Eden medical team. The lead physician, Dr. Vasquez, had went to check on the children in theColony Shipbefore their departure, and was left on board. Julia Heller is the daughter of Councilwoman Heller. She is also a "chromo-tilt" being that her genetics were skewed to give her an advantage in the medical arts. In addition to being a doctor, she often teamed up with Yale to find scientific answers to the colonists' problems. Julia is very distant and professional at first, but gradually warms toward the other crew members. She is also carrying a secret - she is a spy forThe Council, who are trying to find a way of controlling theTerriansso they can populate G889. Julia is forced by the Council's representative,Reilly, to perform experiments onUlysses Adair, and to inform on her fellow crew members. Whey they discover her secret she is banished from the group, they later accept her back into the group when she proves her loyalty and leaves her allegiance with the Council behind. While on G889, Julia also fell in love withAlonzo Solace.

She's a background character with few lines, but since the cast is limited to the 2 dozen survivors of the crash she appears in most episodes of the series for continuity.

characters.name: Mary
characters.description: MaryRelativesTerriansUnnamed MotherUnnamed FatherPortrayed byKelli WilliamsFirstappearanceMoon CrossMary is a human girl who was taken in by the terrians after renegades killed her parents. Unnamed MotherUnnamed Father Mary was played by Kelli Williams.

characters.name: Morgan Martin
characters.description: Morgan MartinRelativesBess Martin(wife)Portrayed byJohn GegenhuberFirstappearanceFirst ContactThe Government Liaison,Morgan, and his Earth-raised wife,Bess Martin, are the only married couple on G889. They were married approximately two years when they left the Stations. While total opposites, Morgan and Bess are deeply committed to each other. Morgan was a "Level Four" out of the six levels needed for clearance. He is obsessed with making a fortune, presumably so he can provide Bess with everything she never had on Earth.

characters.name: Morgan Martin

characters.name: Reilly
characters.description: ReillyRelativesDr. Franklin Bennett ("father")Portrayed byTerry O'QuinnFirstappearanceWaterReillywas Julia's contact when she was spying for the Council. She never spoke to him in person, only in VR. Reilly was supposedly sent by the Council to monitorG88-9, and to try to control theTerrians, who are the guardians of the planet. He was especially interested in the changes toUlysses Adair. At one point, Reilly forced Julia to attempt to remove Uly's pineal gland. After a communication from the Terrians, she lied to Reilly to protect Uly. Julia believed that he was a real person, located somewhere either on the planet or in orbit.  In the last episode, however, Reilly was discovered to be part of a sophisticated biological monitoring program called EVE. EVE was created and set into orbit by Dr. Franklin Bennett, a Council scientist sent to explore G889 and report his findings. The Colonists discovered Reilly's true nature when the EVE computer began to malfunction, causing them to become ill. With Yale's help, the colonists "reset" EVE, and Reilly was back to his old malevolent self.

characters.name: True Danziger
characters.description: True DanzigerRelativesJohn Danziger(father)Portrayed byJ. Madison WrightFirstappearanceFirst ContactJohn Danziger's eleven-year-old daughter,Trueis an inquisitive tomboy who is more comfortable around tools than books. She began helping her father out on jobs at an early age, and is the darling of the Operations crew. Like most girls, she likes small animals - especially cats. She secretly adopts aKoba, with disastrous results. She is also very trusting of strangers, and jealous of her father's newfound responsibilities - which leads to disaster when the group encounters a penal colonist namedGaal.

characters.name: Ulysses Adair
characters.description: Ulysses AdairRelativesDevon Adair(mother)Portrayed byJoey ZimmermanFirstappearanceFirst ContactA child suffering from theSyndrome:Ulysses Adairis the son ofDevon Adair, who founded the Eden Project in hopes of curing him. He is headstrong, adventurous, and spoiled. Ulysses has lived his short life in an exoskeleton which provides most of his body functions, and keeps him safe from disease. Shortly after the colonists arrive on G889, he is taken underground by the Terrians, who heal him. Uly then becomes a link between the Humans and Terrians.

characters.description: WalmanPortrayed byWalter NormanWalmanwas a member of the ops crew serving underJohn Danziger. He's a background character with few lines, but since the cast is limited to the 2 dozen survivors of the crash he appears in most episodes of the series for continuity. He knows how to handle a rifle so is often on guard duty, he can usually be seen during standoffs withGrendlersandTerrians.

characters.name: Yale
characters.description: Brayden CroixPortrayed bySullivan WalkerFirstappearanceFirst ContactUly'stutor, andDevon Adair's closest advisor,Yaleis a former criminal who was converted to a cybernetic tutor by the Council. He is part library researcher, part teacher, part minister, and part father-figure to the colonists. The process erased his memory and implanted knowledge that made him useful as a tutor from the Adair family. Despite the instability of others in the Yale Program, Devon begged her family to let Yale remain with them. As part of his programming Yale has a behavioral modification chip, which gives him an aversion to any type of weapon. When his programming begins to fail, he is discovered to be a former Special Forces soldier who disobeyed orders to shoot unarmed civilians. The Council covered up their own crimes by inducting him into the Yale Program.

characters.description: The Z.E.D. Unit is a technologically modified soldier who was left on G-889 due to faults in his programming.Z.E.D.RelativesUnknownPortrayed byJeff KoberFirstappearanceRedemption
